acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_prose_01_negation_tic] Across the revised lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:117, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:283, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:295, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:313, no more than one major emotional or revelatory beat may rely on an opening negative-definition phrase such as 'it was not', 'not X exactly', or 'never only'. If three or more cited beats still use that same template, fail.

[chapter_01_prose_02_clipped_narration] At the revised passages anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:19 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:217, each emphasis beat must remain brief and forceful but read as a complete sentence aloud, with an explicit subject and predicate. If either beat still depends on a fragment created by dropping the subject or verb for effect, fail.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:117, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:283, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:295, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:313, no more than one major emotional or revelatory beat may rely on an opening negative-definition phrase such as 'it was not', 'not X exactly', or 'never only'. If three or more cited beats still use that same template, fail.
findings.problem: Charged beats keep arriving through the same negative-definition construction: the prose repeatedly tells the reader what a touch, feeling, or revelation is not before naming what it is. After this many recurrences in one chapter, the pattern reads as a sentence-level tic rather than an intentional echo and starts flattening discovery.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated negation beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:117, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:283, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:295, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:313 so most of them define the moment positively through action, sensation, or image on the first pass. Keep the single strongest negation if you want the echo; cut the recurrence everywhere else.

findings.acceptance_test: At the revised passages anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:19 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:217, each emphasis beat must remain brief and forceful but read as a complete sentence aloud, with an explicit subject and predicate. If either beat still depends on a fragment created by dropping the subject or verb for effect, fail.
findings.problem: The chapter slips into clipped narration that drops a predicate or subject for emphasis ('Then immediately, angrily, Thea.' / 'Saw, Cora knew...'). The constitution explicitly disallows elliptical clipping in narration, and these fragments stand out because the surrounding prose is otherwise controlled and grammatical.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the clipped narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:19 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:217 into fully grammatical sentences that keep the same speed and pressure without omitting the subject or predicate. Check the nearby emphasis lines in those same beats so the revision does not simply relocate the clipping into the next sentence.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into queer secrecy, erotic specificity, dust-sick bodies, economic shame, bad bargains, and the humiliations of dependence. When deciding between a tidier scene and a truer one, choose the version that preserves historical pressure and emotional contradiction even if it leaves characters compromised, unsympathetic, or exposed.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If grief, desire, debt, illness, bodily damage, or shame are abstracted at the moment they should become sensory and social, rewrite toward the concrete consequence until the scene stops hiding from itself.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: moderate-high - contractions are the baseline for speech; uncontracted forms should appear only for emphasis, social performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural rhythms across the book: survival set-piece, aftermath inventory, intimate chamber scene, bureaucratic squeeze, outdoor search, quiet communal ritual, preparation without payoff, public reckoning, and departure elegy. Vary scene counts, dialogue density, and where the turn comes from so consecutive chapters never feel built from the same dramatic machine.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plainspoken and period-credible, with agricultural, domestic, and biblical undertones used sparingly; favor exact nouns and verbs over decorative frontier lyricism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person rotating among Cora, Thea, Dell, August, and Hester, with only brief collective widening when weather or communal memory demands it
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive prose: longer weather and labor sentences that can suddenly snap short at revelation, pain, or shame.
